div1.entryFree:
Moiety, 1) one of two equal parts, a half: All's III, 2, 69. Wint. III, 2, 40. IV, 4, 842. H5 V, 2, 229. R3 I, 2, 250. II, 2, 60. H8 I, 2, 12. Ant. V, 1, 19. Cymb. I, 4, 118.
2) a portion, part in general: the love I dedicate to your lordship is without end; whereof this pamphlet, without beginning, is but a superfluous m. Lucr. Dedic. Cymb. I, 4, 118 by their verdict is determined the clear eye's m. and the dear heart's part, Sonn. 46, 12. forgive a m. of the principal, Merch. IV, 1, 26. a m. of my rest might come to me again, Wint. II, 3, 8. my m. equals not one of yours, H4A III, 1, 96. pay betimes a m. of that mass of moan to come, Troil. II, 2, 107. a m. competent was gaged by our king, Hml. I, 1, 90. qualities are so weighed, that curiosity in neither can make choice of either's m. Lr. I, 1, 7.
